zh.lifeSummary: 當Johannis Le Carter II於1316年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾貝弗利時，他的父親Johannis le Carter36歲，母親Alicia LeCarter32歲。他於1335年之前與Agnes Brome在英國英格蘭越可爾貝弗利結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1349年住在英國斯塔福爾卡諾可且於1391年住在英國賀特福爾阿波茲 明日香。他在英國越可爾博爾頓 珀西被列為tailor - all saints parish。他去世於1393年在英國英格蘭賀特福爾聖 阿爾班斯享年77歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀特福爾聖 阿爾班斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Johannis Le Carter II于1316年出生在英国英格兰越可爾貝弗利时，他的父亲Johannis le Carter36岁，母亲Alicia LeCarter32岁。他于1335年之前与Agnes Brome在英国英格兰越可爾貝弗利结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1349年住在英国斯塔福爾卡諾可且于1391年住在英国賀特福爾阿波茲 明日香。他在英国越可爾博爾頓 珀西被列为tailor - all saints parish。他去世于1393年在英国英格兰賀特福爾聖 阿爾班斯享年77岁，埋葬在英国英格兰賀特福爾聖 阿爾班斯。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Carter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a transporter of goods, from Middle English carter(e) ‘carter’ (Anglo-Norman French car(e)tier, Old French charetier, medieval Latin carettarius, carettator). The Old French word coalesced with the earlier Middle English word cart(e) ‘cart’, which is from either Old Norse kartr or Old English cræt, both of which, like the Late Latin word, were probably derived from Celtic. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: shortened form of   McCarter  .
Americanized form of German   Karter   ‘carder’.

variants.name: Charman
meanings: English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for a carter, carrier, or coachman, from Middle English (Old French) char, car ‘car, cart, carriage’ + Middle English man ‘man’. Compare Carter and Carman 2.

English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for someone who did odd jobs, from Middle English char(re), chere (Old English cier(r), cer(r), cear(r), cær(r)) ‘turn (of activity), small job, chore’ + man. Compare Carman 2.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of Slovenian Čarman (see Carman 5).

variants.name: Chartier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Adelard, Alcide, Emile, Gilles, Raoul, Rosaire, Alphonse.
French: shortened form of charretier, an occupational name for a carter, from an agent derivative of Old French charette ‘cart’.
French Canadian: altered form of English   Carter  , a cognate of 1 above. Compare   Sharkey   and   Shorkey  .

variants.name: Karter
meanings: German: occupational name for a carder (someone who prepares wool for spinning), from Middle High German karter. Compare Carter .

variants.name: McCarter
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   McArthur  .

variants.name: Carrier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Cecile, Alain, Gaetan, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Normand.

English and southern French: from Middle English, Old French car(r)ier (from Late Latin carrarius, a derivative of carrum ‘cart, wagon’, a word of Gaulish origin), an occupational name for a carter (see Carter ), in French also for a cartwright. Compare French Charrier .
French: occupational name for a stonemason or quarryman, carrier. Compare Coyer .

variants.name: Cartman
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): occupational name from Middle English cartman ‘man who operated a cart for the transport of goods’. Compare Carter .
Americanized form of German Kartmann (see Kartman ).

nameMeanings.name: Johannis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Johannis Le Carter II was born in 1316, in Beverley, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Johannis le Carter, was 36 and his mother, Alicia LeCarter, was 32. He married Agnes Brome before 1335, in Beverley, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He lived in Cannock, Staffordshire, England in 1349 and Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, England in 1391. His occupation is listed as tailor - all saints parish in Bolton Percy, Yorkshire, England. He died in 1393, in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 77, and was buried in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Johannis Le Carter II est né en 1316, à Beverley, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Johannis le Carter, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Alicia LeCarter, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Brome avant 1335, à Beverley, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il a vécu à Cannock, Staffordshire, Angleterre en 1349 et à Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, Angleterre en 1391. Sa profession de son indiquée est tailor - all saints parish à Bolton Percy, Everwyck, Angleterre. Il est décédé en 1393, à St Albans, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterré à St Albans, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat vor 1335 in Beverley, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Agnes Brome geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1349 hat er in Cannock, Staffordshire, England und 1391 in Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, England gelebt. sein Beruf ist als tailor - all saints parish in Bolton Percy, Yorkshire, England aufgeführt. Er starb 1393 im Alter von 77 Jahren in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Johannis Le Carter II è nato il nel 1316 nel Beverley, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Johannis le Carter, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Alicia LeCarter, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Agnes Brome prima del 1335, nel Beverley, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Vissuto a Cannock, Staffordshire, England nel 1349 e nel Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, England nel 1391. La sua occupazione è indicata come tailor - all saints parish nel Bolton Percy, Yorkshire, England. Morì nel 1393, nel St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 77 anni, e fu sepolto a St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Johannis Le Carter IIが1316年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアベバーリーで生まれた時，父Johannis le Carterは36歳，母Alicia LeCarterは32歳であった。1335年以前にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアベバーリーでAgnes Bromeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1349年にはイギリススタフォードシュアイアカノックに，1391年にはイギリスハートフォードシュアイアアボツ ラングリーに住んでいた。イギリスヨークシュアイアボルトン ペルシでの職業はtailor - all saints parishと記録されている。彼は1393年にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアスト アルバンスで77歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアスト アルバンスにて埋葬された。

ko.Barton St. David, Somerset, England: 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 바턴 스트 데이비드
ko.lifeSummary: Johannis Le Carter II(이)가 1316년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 베벌리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Johannis le Carter(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Alicia LeCarter(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1335년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 베벌리에서 Agnes Brome(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1349년도에 영국 스태퍼드실르 캔옥크에서, 1391년도에 영국 허트퍼드실르 애벗스 랭글리에서 살았다. 그의 직업은 영국 요르크실르 볼턴 퍼시에서 tailor - all saints parish이었다. 그는 1393년 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 스트 알반스에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 스트 알반스에 묻혔다.

pt.Barton St. David, Somerset, England: Barton St David, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Johannis Le Carter II nasceu em 1316, em Beverley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Johannis le Carter, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Alicia LeCarter, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes Brome antes 1335, em Beverley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele viveu em Cannock, Staffordshire, Inglaterra em 1349 e em Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra em 1391. Sua profissão aparece como tailor - all saints parish em Bolton Percy, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 1393, em St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 77 anos, e foi sepultado em St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Barton St. David, Somerset, England: бартон ст дэвид, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Johannis Le Carter II родился в 1316 г. в местности бивирли, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Johannis le Carter было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Alicia LeCarter, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Brome перед 1335 г. в местности бивирли, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он жил в местности каннокк, стаффордшир, Англия в 1349 году и в местности эббоц лангли, хиртфордшир, Англия в 1391 году.  Он работал по профессии tailor - all saints parish в местности болтон пирси, еркшир, Англия. Он умер в 1393 г. в местности ст албанс, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 77 лет и был похоронен в местности ст албанс, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Barton St. David, Somerset, England: Barton St David, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Johannis Le Carter II nació en 1316, en Beverley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Johannis le Carter, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Alicia LeCarter, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Agnes Brome antes de 1335, en Beverley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Vivió en Cannock, Staffordshire, Inglaterra en 1349 y en Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra en 1391. Su ocupación registrada era tailor - all saints parish en Bolton Percy, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Murió en 1393, en St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 77 años, y fue sepultado en St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
